reason: The item cleanly tests boundary-aware admissible inference from a small set of experimental observations, especially non-monotonicity between injection mass, cooling rate, and peak temperature, while also separating mechanism vs overreach claims. Decoys are largely single-fault and engineering-plausible (monotonicity guarantee, universal generalization, and internal-TR suppression), producing meaningful discrimination. Minor ambiguity remains because some correct choices use “consistent with” mechanistic language that could invite over-interpretation, and two decoys lean on similarly broad overgeneralization instincts, slightly reducing separability.

The item cleanly tests SOC-dependent admissible inference about WM suppression boundaries (prevention vs delay vs mitigation) using multiple quantitative cues, and the correct set is tightly supported by those cues without requiring unstated domain assumptions. Decoys are largely single-fault and engineering-plausible (direction error, causal overclaim, and explicit contradiction), though one correct option (C) introduces a mild implicit assumption about reduced peak surface temperature translating to reduced propagation risk, slightly weakening neutrality and boundary tightness.
